purpose.summary: CBPAUP0C is a batch program that purges expired authorization records from the PAUTSUM0 and PAUTDTL1 IMS segments. It reads authorization summary records and then iterates through the associated detail records, deleting those that have expired based on a configurable expiry period. The program also takes checkpoints periodically to ensure recoverability.
purpose.business_context: This program maintains the authorization database by removing old and irrelevant authorization records, ensuring efficient storage and processing.

inputs.name: PAUTSUM0
inputs.description: Pending Authorization Summary segment, containing summary information for authorization requests.

inputs.name: PAUTDTL1
inputs.description: Pending Authorization Details segment, containing detailed information for individual authorization requests.

inputs.name: SYSIN
inputs.description: Input parameter string containing expiry days, checkpoint frequency, debug flag and checkpoint display frequency.

business_rules.description: Authorization details are considered expired if the difference between the current date and the authorization date exceeds the configured expiry days.
business_rules.logic_summary: Calculates the difference between the current date and the authorization date and compares it to the expiry days.

business_rules.description: If an authorization detail record is approved (PA-AUTH-RESP-CODE = '00') and is expired, the approved authorization count and amount in the summary record are decremented.
business_rules.logic_summary: Checks the authorization response code and decrements the appropriate counters in the summary record.

business_rules.description: If an authorization detail record is declined (PA-AUTH-RESP-CODE not = '00') and is expired, the declined authorization count and amount in the summary record are decremented.
business_rules.logic_summary: Checks the authorization response code and decrements the appropriate counters in the summary record.

transforms.transformation_description: Calculates the authorization date by subtracting PA-AUTH-DATE-9C from 99999.
Calculates the difference between the current day of the year and the calculated authorization date.

copybooks_used.purpose: Defines the structure of the Pending Authorization Summary IMS segment.
Defines the structure of the Pending Authorization Details IMS segment.

paragraphs.purpose: This is the program entry point. It calls CIPAUSMY and CIPAUDTY, but the purpose of these calls is unknown as the source code is not provided. It is the first paragraph executed when the program starts.

This paragraph controls the main processing loop of the program. It first performs 1000-INITIALIZE to initialize variables and read parameters. Then, it enters a loop that continues until either an error flag is set (ERR-FLG-ON) or the end of the authorization database is reached (END-OF-AUTHDB). Inside the loop, it retrieves authorization detail records using 3000-FIND-NEXT-AUTH-DTL and checks if each record is expired using 4000-CHECK-IF-EXPIRED. If a record is expired, it is deleted using 5000-DELETE-AUTH-DTL. After processing all detail records for a summary record, it checks if both approved and declined authorization counts are zero. If so, it deletes the summary record using 6000-DELETE-AUTH-SUMMARY. Periodically, based on WS-AUTH-SMRY-PROC-CNT and P-CHKP-FREQ, it takes a checkpoint using 9000-TAKE-CHECKPOINT. Finally, it displays summary statistics before terminating.

This paragraph initializes program variables and reads input parameters. It accepts the current date and day of the year. It reads parameters from SYSIN into PRM-INFO and displays them. It then checks if the expiry days (P-EXPIRY-DAYS) is numeric; if so, it moves it to WS-EXPIRY-DAYS; otherwise, it defaults to 5. It also checks if the checkpoint frequency (P-CHKP-FREQ) is spaces, 0, or LOW-VALUES; if so, it defaults to 5. Similarly, it checks the checkpoint display frequency (P-CHKP-DIS-FREQ) and defaults to 10 if it's spaces, 0, or LOW-VALUES. Finally, it checks the debug flag (P-DEBUG-FLAG) and defaults to 'N' if it's not 'Y'.
This paragraph is a standard exit point for the 1000-INITIALIZE paragraph. It simply contains the EXIT statement, which returns control to the calling paragraph.

This paragraph reads the next authorization summary record from the PAUTSUM0 IMS segment. If the debug flag is on, it displays the current summary read count. It uses the EXEC DLI GN command to retrieve the next segment. The DIBSTAT value is then evaluated. If it's spaces, the NOT-END-OF-AUTHDB flag is set to TRUE, the summary read count (WS-NO-SUMRY-READ) and the authorization summary process count (WS-AUTH-SMRY-PROC-CNT) are incremented, and the account ID (PA-ACCT-ID) is moved to WS-CURR-APP-ID. If DIBSTAT is 'GB', the END-OF-AUTHDB flag is set to TRUE. If DIBSTAT is any other value, an error message is displayed, and the program abends via 9999-ABEND.
This paragraph is a standard exit point for the 2000-FIND-NEXT-AUTH-SUMMARY paragraph. It simply contains the EXIT statement, which returns control to the calling paragraph.

This paragraph reads the next authorization detail record from the PAUTDTL1 IMS segment. If the debug flag is on, it displays the current detail read count. It uses the EXEC DLI GNP command to retrieve the next segment. The DIBSTAT value is then evaluated. If it's spaces, the MORE-AUTHS flag is set to TRUE, and the detail read count (WS-NO-DTL-READ) is incremented. If DIBSTAT is 'GE' or 'GB', the NO-MORE-AUTHS flag is set to TRUE. If DIBSTAT is any other value, an error message is displayed, and the program abends via 9999-ABEND.
This paragraph is a standard exit point for the 3000-FIND-NEXT-AUTH-DTL paragraph. It simply contains the EXIT statement, which returns control to the calling paragraph.

This paragraph checks if an authorization detail record is expired. It calculates the authorization date (WS-AUTH-DATE) by subtracting the PA-AUTH-DATE-9C from 99999. It then calculates the difference (WS-DAY-DIFF) between the current day of the year and the calculated authorization date. If the difference is greater than or equal to the expiry days (WS-EXPIRY-DAYS), the QUALIFIED-FOR-DELETE flag is set to TRUE. If the authorization response code is '00', the approved authorization count and amount in the summary record are decremented. Otherwise, the declined authorization count and amount are decremented. If the record is not expired, the NOT-QUALIFIED-FOR-DELETE flag is set to TRUE.
This paragraph is a standard exit point for the 4000-CHECK-IF-EXPIRED paragraph. It simply contains the EXIT statement, which returns control to the calling paragraph.

error_handling.condition: DIBSTAT not equal to ' ' or 'GB' in 2000-FIND-NEXT-AUTH-SUMMARY
DIBSTAT not equal to ' ' or 'GE' or 'GB' in 3000-FIND-NEXT-AUTH-DTL
